name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-98
title: dispatcher hooks fail at runtime — _dispatcher_lib not deployed
description: Plan C deployed stop-dispatcher.py / session-start-dispatcher.py / user-prompt-submit-dispatcher.py to .claude/hooks/ but did not deploy their shared helper _dispatcher_lib.py alongside. Dispatchers add their own directory to sys.path and then `from _dispatcher_lib import ...`, but the helper only exists at .claude/features/rabbit-cage/hooks/ (the source location). Every Claude Code session in any clone after Plan C is degraded — hooks emit a non-blocking error on every event; no drift detection, no scope alerts, no welcome banner, no policy refresh fires. Plan C's test suite only exercised the source library, not the deployed shape, so this was missed. Discovered when ws48 attempted to start Plan D and every hook errored. Fix candidates: (a) add _dispatcher_lib.py as a publish_file entry in rabbit-cage's manifest so it deploys alongside the dispatchers; (b) change dispatcher imports to point at the absolute source path via sys.path.insert(repo_root/.claude/features/rabbit-cage/hooks). (a) is simpler; (b) avoids deploying internal helpers. Either way the test suite must grow an end-to-end test that invokes the deployed hooks. Blocks: Plan D and all subsequent waves.

history.note: Plan C fix-up wave: added publish_file entry for hooks/_dispatcher_lib.py so the helper deploys alongside the dispatchers; added end-to-end test-deployed-hooks-execute.py that runs install.run_publish_loop and subprocess-invokes every deployed dispatcher; added spec invariant 12 to pin deploy-alongside requirement; bumped rabbit-cage 5.0.0 -> 5.1.0.
